fresh_facts.claim: The Washington Post reported that US and Israeli forces struck four key Iranian ballistic missile production facilities and 29 other targets on March 29, 2026.
Over 600 Israeli Defense Force strikes targeted Iranian ballistic missile sites and production facilities in Esfahan Province, including the main explosives factory, between March 24-26, 2026.
Iranian forces launched ballistic missiles at a Peshmerga base near Erbil on March 24, 2026, killing 6 and wounding 30.
Iranian forces struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on March 24-26, 2026, wounding 15 or more US troops.

Israeli Defense Force operations in southern Lebanon against Hezbollah killed 8 fighters and included plans to intensify ground operations with additional brigades like the 300th Infantry between March 24-26, 2026.
The United States postponed strikes on Iran's energy facilities, announcing the revised date as April 6, 2026, on March 26.
Combined US-Israeli forces struck over 8,000 Iranian targets by late March 2026, including the Natanz nuclear facility on March 21.
Iran's ballistic missile launches declined significantly by late March 2026 due to depletion of missile stocks.
Thailand secured an agreement with Iran on March 28, 2026, for safe passage of Thai oil vessels through the Strait of Hormuz.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was appointed as Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council on March 28, 2026, shifting decision-making toward military-security structures.
Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani and security official Ali Larijani were killed on March 17, 2026, with succession mechanisms continuing to function despite the losses.
Nationwide internet blackouts have occurred in Iran during the 2026 conflict, described as one of the longest on record.
Strait of Hormuz shipping disruptions have created global economic fallout and market reshaping as of March 30, 2026.
US-Israeli strikes have targeted nuclear, military, intelligence, and industrial sites across Iranian provinces including Tehran as of March 28, 2026.
The Iran-US conflict that began February 28, 2026, continued with sustained combined force operations through late March 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: US-Israeli forces have conducted over 8,000 combined strikes through late March 2026, including destruction of nuclear facilities at Natanz and four ballistic missile production facilities on March 29, while Iranian missile stocks are depleted and military leadership has been reshuffled toward security-dominated structures amid succession uncertainty. Nationwide internet blackouts persist as Strait of Hormuz disruptions create global economic fallout, while Iran continues ballistic missile launches against regional targets despite diminished capacity.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Combined US-Israeli forces executed over 8,000 strikes by late March 2026, including strikes on Natanz nuclear facility on March 21 and four ballistic missile production facilities on March 29, severely degrading Iranian military-industrial capacity.
